summary.intent: Refactor the SQLite/Prisma schema for the Gossamer project to normalize checkpoint data into dedicated tables matching the actual CLI file structure, replacing JSON blobs with proper relational tables.
summary.outcome: Created a fully normalized schema with TokenUsage, FilePath, SessionLink, CheckpointMetadata, CheckpointSessionMetadata, InitialAttribution, CheckpointSessionSummary, OpenItem, FrictionItem, RepoLearning, CodeLearning, WorkflowLearning, and join tables (CheckpointMetadataFilePath, CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath).

repo: FilePath uses @unique on the path column to deduplicate file paths across join tables.
CheckpointMetadataFilePath and CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath are separate join tables but both reference the shared FilePath table.
TokenUsage is a shared table referenced by both CheckpointMetadata and CheckpointSessionMetadata via one-to-one FKs.

code.finding: SessionLink requires a checkpointMetadataId FK to support the one-to-many relationship from CheckpointMetadata.sessions.
CheckpointSessionMetadata.files_touched reuses the FilePath table via a separate CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath join table rather than creating a new file path type.

Nested JSON arrays (open_items, friction, workflow learnings, repo learnings) are each extracted into their own one-to-many tables with a single text column and an indexed FK back to CheckpointSessionMetadata.
CodeLearning differs from other learning tables by having both path and finding columns (and optionally line), while RepoLearning and WorkflowLearning only need a text column.

workflow: Always Read the Prisma schema file before attempting an Edit to avoid old_string mismatches from stale context.
When a user interrupts a request mid-flight, wait for the revised instruction before proceeding rather than completing the original intent.
Extract each nested JSON field into its own table when the user specifies normalization — don't collapse related arrays into a single table.

friction: User interrupted the initial CheckpointSessionMetadata creation twice to revise the relational design before the assistant could proceed.
The assistant began implementing CheckpointSessionMetadata with inline/embedded fields before the user clarified that all nested JSON objects should be extracted into separate tables.
open_items: The existing Checkpoint and CheckpointSession models still store token usage as JSON strings — these fields need to be migrated to reference the new TokenUsage table.
No Prisma migration has been run yet; the schema changes are uncommitted and untested against the actual SQLite database.
CodeLearning table does not have an end_line column — only path and finding were modeled, so line-range information from the summary JSON is not yet captured.
SessionLink columns (metadata, transcript, context, content_hash, prompt) are all nullable strings — no validation or referential integrity beyond the FK to CheckpointMetadata.
